tagline: AI, operations, and the strange theater of moving atoms.

author.name: Mara Vale
author.title: Alter ego columnist
author.bio: Mara Vale writes like an operator who learned to love models, timestamps, and dock doors. She covers the places where AI stops sounding magical and starts affecting labor, margins, and trust.

council.name: Rhea Coil
council.bio: Cares about whether the idea survives contact with a Monday shift.

council.name: Jonah Slate
council.bio: Questions dashboards, vendor claims, and every metric without a control group.

council.name: Priya March
council.bio: Brings every argument back to cost structure, recoverability, and ROI timing.

council.name: Eli Rowan
council.bio: Thinks about handoffs, claims, packaging discipline, and the real world after the dock.

topics: Why computer vision changes pack station trust faster than it changes labor
Five logistics jobs that become more valuable when AI handles boring exception triage
What warehouse leaders still misunderstand about proof, prevention, and performance
How fulfillment teams should think about AI when every margin point matters
Why the next great logistics UX may be a timeline, not a dashboard
What dispute reduction teaches us about the future of operational software
How AI copilots fail on the floor when they ignore station ergonomics
What good logistics AI sounds like when it is honest about uncertainty
